zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean IV d'Harcourt於1299年11月9日出生在法國諾門迪艾哈爾庫特時，他的父親Jean d'Harcourt III24歲，母親Alix de Brabant25歲。他於1315年7月22日與Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1346年8月26日在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆阿別維爾享年46歲，埋葬在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean IV d'Harcourt于1299年11月9日出生在法国諾門迪艾哈爾庫特时，他的父亲Jean d'Harcourt III24岁，母亲Alix de Brabant25岁。他于1315年7月22日与Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1346年8月26日在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆阿別維爾享年46岁，埋葬在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆可雷希--朋提額。

nameMeanings.name: d'Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean IV d'Harcourt was born on 9 November 1299, in Harcourt, Eure, Normandy, France, his father, Jean d'Harcourt III, was 24 and his mother, Alix de Brabant, was 25. He married Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque on 22 July 1315, in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 26 August 1346, in Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 46, and was buried in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean IV d'Harcourt est né le 9 novembre 1299, à Harcourt, Eure, Normandie, France, son père, Jean d'Harcourt III, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alix de Brabant, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque le 22 juillet 1315, à Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 26 août 1346, à Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean IV d'Harcourt am 9. November 1299 in Harcourt, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean d'Harcourt III 24 und seine Mutter Alix de Brabant 25. Er hat am 22. Juli 1315 in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 26. August 1346 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean IV d'Harcourt è nato il il 9 novembre 1299 nel Harcourt, Eure, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Jean d'Harcourt III, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Alix de Brabant, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque il 22 luglio 1315, nel Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 26 agosto 1346, nel Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean IV d'Harcourtが1299年11月9日にフランスノーマンディエウアハーコウルトで生まれた時，父Jean d'Harcourt IIIは24歳，母Alix de Brabantは25歳であった。1315年7月22日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメクルeシ-エン-プオンスイエウでIsabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêqueと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1346年8月26日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメアベヴィルで46歳で死去し，フランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメクルeシ-エン-プオンスイエウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean IV d'Harcourt(이)가 1299년 11월 9일 프랑스 노먼디 우르 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean d'Harcourt III(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de Brabant(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1315년 7월 22일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 크e시--폰스이외에서 Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1346년 8월 26일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 애브베빌에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 크e시--폰스이외에 묻혔다.

mn.9 novembre 1299–26 August 1346: 1299 оны Арваннэгдүгээр сарын 9–1346 оны Наймдугаар сарын 26

pt.9 novembre 1299–26 August 1346: 9 de novembro de 1299–26 de agosto de 1346
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean IV d'Harcourt nasceu em 9 de novembro de 1299, em Harcourt, Eure, Normandie, França, seu pai, Jean d'Harcourt III, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Brabant, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque em 22 de julho de 1315, em Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de agosto de 1346, em Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean IV d'Harcourt родился 9 ноября 1299 г. в местности харкоурт, эур, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean d'Harcourt III было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alix de Brabant, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque 22 июля 1315 г. в местности крeси-ен-понтиеу, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 26 августа 1346 г. в местности эббевилл, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности крeси-ен-понтиеу, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.9 novembre 1299–26 August 1346: 9 de noviembre de 1299–26 de agosto de 1346
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean IV d'Harcourt nació el 9 de noviembre de 1299, en Harcourt, Eure, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Jean d'Harcourt III, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Alix de Brabant, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Isabeau de Parthenay l'Archevêque el 22 de julio de 1315, en Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 26 de agosto de 1346, en Abbeville, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Crécy-en-Ponthieu, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia.
